paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now Korah, the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, with Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab, and On, the son of Peleth, sons of Reuben, took [men]:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and they rose up before Moses, with certain of the children of Israel, two hundred and fifty princes of the congregation, called to the assembly, men of renown;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and they assembled themselves together against Moses and against Aaron, and said unto them, Ye take too much upon you, seeing all the congregation are holy, every one of them, and Jehovah is among them: wherefore then lift ye up yourselves above the assembly of Jehovah?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when Moses heard it, he fell upon his face:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and he spake unto Korah and unto all his company, saying, In the morning Jehovah will show who are his, and who is holy, and will cause him to come near unto him: even him whom he shall choose will he cause to come near unto him.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: This do: take you censers, Korah, and all his company;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and put fire in them, and put incense upon them before Jehovah to-morrow: and it shall be that the man whom Jehovah doth choose, he [shall be] holy: ye take too much upon you, ye sons of Levi.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses said unto Korah, Hear now, ye sons of Levi:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: [seemeth it but] a small thing unto you, that the God of Israel hath separated you from the congregation of Israel, to bring you near to himself, to do the service of the tabernacle of Jehovah, and to stand before the congregation to minister unto them;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and that he hath brought thee near, and all thy brethren the sons of Levi with thee? and seek ye the priesthood also?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Therefore thou and all thy company are gathered together against Jehovah: and Aaron, what is he that ye murmur against him?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab; and they said, We will not come up:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: is it a small thing that thou hast brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey, to kill us in the wilderness, but thou must needs make thyself also a prince over us?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Moreover thou hast not brought us into a land flowing with milk and honey, nor given us inheritance of fields and vineyards: wilt thou put out the eyes of these men? we will not come up.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses was very wroth, and said unto Jehovah, Respect not thou their offering: I have not taken one ass from them, neither have I hurt one of them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses said unto Korah, Be thou and all thy company before Jehovah, thou, and they, and Aaron, to-morrow:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and take ye every man his censer, and put incense upon them, and bring ye before Jehovah every man his censer, two hundred and fifty censers; thou also, and Aaron, each his censer.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they took every man his censer, and put fire in them, and laid incense thereon, and stood at the door of the tent of meeting with Moses and Aaron.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Korah assembled all the congregation against them unto the door of the tent of meeting: and the glory of Jehovah appeared unto all the congregation.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Separate yourselves from among this congregation, that I may consume them in a moment.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they fell upon their faces, and said, O God, the God of the spirits of all flesh, shall one man sin, and wilt thou be wroth with all the congregation?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Speak unto the congregation, saying, Get you up from about the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses rose up and went unto Dathan and Abiram; and the elders of Israel followed him.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he spake unto the congregation, saying, Depart, I pray you, from the tents of these wicked men, and touch nothing of theirs, lest ye be consumed in all their sins.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: So they gat them up from the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram, on every side: and Dathan and Abiram came out, and stood at the door of their tents, and their wives, and their sons, and their little ones.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses said, Hereby ye shall know that Jehovah hath sent me to do all these works; for [I have] not [done them] of mine own mind.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: If these men die the common death of all men, or if they be visited after the visitation of all men; then Jehovah hath not sent me.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But if Jehovah make a new thing, and the ground open its mouth, and swallow them up, with all that appertain unto them, and they go down alive into Sheol; then ye shall understand that these men have despised Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And it came to pass, as he made an end of speaking all these words, that the ground clave asunder that was under them;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and the earth opened its mouth, and swallowed them up, and their households, and all the men that appertained unto Korah, and all their goods.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: So they, and all that appertained to them, went down alive into Sheol: and the earth closed upon them, and they perished from among the assembly.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And all Israel that were round about them fled at the cry of them; for they said, Lest the earth swallow us up.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And fire came forth from Jehovah, and devoured the two hundred and fifty men that offered the incense.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Speak unto Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, that he take up the censers out of the burning, and scatter thou the fire yonder; for they are holy,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: even the censers of these sinners against their own lives; and let them be made beaten plates for a covering of the altar: for they offered them before Jehovah; therefore they are holy; and they shall be a sign unto the children of Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Eleazar the priest took the brazen censers, which they that were burnt had offered; and they beat them out for a covering of the altar,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: to be a memorial unto the children of Israel, to the end that no stranger, that is not of the seed of Aaron, come near to burn incense before Jehovah; that he be not as Korah, and as his company: as Jehovah spake unto him by Moses.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But on the morrow all the congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron, saying, Ye have killed the people of Jehovah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And it came to pass, when the congregation was assembled against Moses and against Aaron, that they looked toward the tent of meeting: and, behold, the cloud covered it, and the glory of Jehovah appeared.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses and Aaron came to the front of the tent of meeting.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Get you up from among this congregation, that I may consume them in a moment. And they fell upon their faces.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses said unto Aaron, Take they censer, and put fire therein from off the altar, and lay incense thereon, and carry it quickly unto the congregation, and make atonement for them: for there is wrath gone out from Jehovah; the plague is begun.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Aaron took as Moses spake, and ran into the midst of the assembly; and, behold, the plague was begun among the people: and he put on the incense, and made atonement for the people.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he stood between the dead and the living; and the plague was stayed.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Now they that died by the plague were fourteen thousand and seven hundred, besides them that died about the matter of Korah.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Aaron returned unto Moses unto the door of the tent of meeting: and the plague was stayed.
